expectations.text: Recommends `DecodingOutput.descendants()` (transitive walk) over `DecodingOutput.children()` (one-hop) and explains the distinction between the two
expectations.evidence: Section 'Why descendants() and not children()' explains: 'children() — only the one-hop children... descendants() — the transitive walk.'

expectations.text: Notes that `DecodingOutput` is a merge master, so downstream consumers FK to the master itself, not to its part tables (e.g. ClusterlessDecodingV1)
expectations.evidence: States: 'DecodingOutput is a merge master... By Spyglass convention, downstream consumers FK to the merge master itself (DecodingOutput), not to the part tables.'
